title: Practical AI for one-person teams
headline: Practical AI for one-person teams.
description: Dabblewith.ai issue 001: three practical workflows, two community asks, and one honest experiment update for one-person teams.
summary: Three workflows, two community asks, and one honest experiment update.

workflows.label: workflow 1
workflows.title: Founder weekly metrics
workflows.body: Turn weekly founder updates into a 6-line build-in-public scorecard with inputs, decisions, and a human review point.

workflows.label: workflow 2
workflows.title: Meeting notes to action tracker
workflows.body: Convert messy meeting notes into owners, due dates, risks, and follow-ups without storing private transcripts publicly.

workflows.label: workflow 3
workflows.title: Creator repurposing
workflows.body: Take one source asset and draft four channel-specific outputs while preserving your own voice and review standards.

asks.title: What is your hard never-automate line?
asks.body: Reply with one task you do not want an AI system doing without human review. We will turn the best answers into moderation rules.

asks.title: Submit one workflow you already use
asks.body: Send a practical workflow with the private details removed. We will only publish after contributor approval.

experiment: The site has seed workflows and a newsletter signup path, but the honest baseline is still early: no paid revenue, no fake user claims, and only intent signals that can be measured safely.

title: From pages to a measurable AI Builder's Exchange
headline: From pages to a measurable AI Builder's Exchange.
description: Dabblewith.ai issue 002: templates, social cards, conversion dashboard, moderation queue, and one honest build update.
summary: Templates, social cards, conversion dashboard, and moderation path.

workflows.label: workflow 1
workflows.title: Fork a workflow with a worksheet
workflows.body: The top five workflows now have downloadable Markdown templates with safe input, human review, and never-automate sections.

workflows.label: workflow 2
workflows.title: Measure CTA intent without PII
workflows.body: The metrics dashboard maps events to weekly decisions while excluding message bodies, emails, phone numbers, raw URLs, user IDs, IPs, and device identifiers.

workflows.label: workflow 3
workflows.title: Moderate community submissions
workflows.body: Submissions get staged, redacted, scored for safety, drafted, approved by the contributor, then published. No auto-publish.

asks.title: What should be the first contributed workflow category?
asks.body: Reply with one category you would actually read: founder ops, creator workflows, no-code automation, research, healthcare/business ops, or WhatsApp community operations.

asks.title: What changed since issue #001?
asks.body: We shipped more surfaces, not fake traction. The useful signal is now clear: worksheet copies, workflow submissions, newsletter clicks, and community-bot setup conversations.

experiment: MRR remains $0 until real paid demand appears. The next measurable loop is contributed workflow intake, moderation, archive mentions, and newsletter signups.
